QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_89

question.text: How are Tier II withdrawal gains taxed?

answer.text: Tax-free like Tier I lump sum
At applicable income tax slab rates

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NPS Tier II withdrawal gains are taxable at the subscriber's applicable income tax slab rates. There are no special concessional rates. **The Trap:** Unlike Tier I where 60% lump sum is tax-free, Tier II gains have no special tax treatment. They are added to total income. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tier I: EEE regime (60% lump sum tax-free, annuity purchase tax-free). Tier II: gains fully taxable at slab rates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise clients that Tier II tax treatment is similar to bank FDs or liquid funds. The low cost advantage may offset the tax disadvantage.
